title: Velvety Peruvian Almond Custard
description: A light, creamy Peruvian dessert with a subtle almond flavor, made entirely with plant-based ingredients for a jain-friendly treat.
instructions: Heat the oat milk and coconut milk in a medium saucepan over medium heat until warm.
Add the sugar and stir until it completely dissolves.
Whisk the cornstarch with 2 tablespoons of cold water to make a smooth slurry.
Pour the slurry into the saucepan, whisking continuously, and cook until the mixture thickens to a custard consistency (about 5-7 minutes).
Remove from heat and stir in the almond extract, vanilla extract, ground cardamom, and lemon juice.
Let the custard cool to room temperature, then divide it among 4 ramekins or glasses.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours to set and develop the flavors.
Just before serving, sprinkle the toasted almonds on top.
